description_for_human: Search documents, run playbooks, manage matters and tasks with Spaarke AI.
description_for_model: This plugin connects to the Spaarke legal document management platform. Use it when the user asks about legal documents, matters, tasks, deadlines, playbook analysis, financial summaries, or communications. All data is scoped to what the authenticated user is permitted to access.

functions.name: semanticSearch
functions.description: Call this when the user wants to find documents by topic, name, content, or description. Performs hybrid semantic and keyword search across all documents the user has access to.

functions.name: listDocuments
functions.description: Call this when the user wants to see a list of documents, optionally filtered by matter, project, or document type.

functions.name: getDocument
functions.description: Call this when the user asks for details about a specific document, such as its metadata, type, or matter association.

functions.name: getDocumentPreviewUrl
functions.description: Call this when the user wants to view or preview a document. Returns a URL that opens the document in a browser.

functions.name: summarizeFile
functions.description: Call this when the user wants a summary of a document's content or key points.

functions.name: listChatPlaybooks
functions.description: Call this when the user asks what analysis playbooks or AI capabilities are available for chat.

functions.name: listPublicPlaybooks
functions.description: Call this when the user asks 'What analysis tools are available?' or wants to browse all playbooks.

functions.name: executePlaybook
functions.description: Call this when the user wants to run a document analysis playbook on a specific document.

functions.name: getPlaybookRunStatus
functions.description: Call this to check the progress of a running playbook execution.

functions.name: getPlaybookRunDetail
functions.description: Call this when a playbook execution has completed and the user wants to see the full results and findings.

functions.name: listEvents
functions.description: Call this when the user asks about their tasks, deadlines, due dates, or assignments. Returns all events — use dueDateTo with a past date and statusCode=1 for overdue items.

functions.name: getEvent
functions.description: Call this when the user asks for details about a specific task, deadline, or event.

functions.name: createEvent
functions.description: Call this when the user wants to create a new task, deadline, or appointment.

functions.name: completeEvent
functions.description: Call this when the user wants to mark a task or event as completed.

functions.name: getPortfolio
functions.description: Call this when the user asks for an overview of their matters, projects, or portfolio.

functions.name: getBriefing
functions.description: Call this when the user asks for their daily briefing or what needs attention today.

functions.name: getMatterAiSummary
functions.description: Call this when the user asks for a summary of a specific matter including recent activity and key documents.

functions.name: getMatterFinanceSummary
functions.description: Call this when the user asks about the budget, spending, or invoices for a matter.

functions.name: searchRecords
functions.description: Call this when the user wants to search for matters, projects, events, or documents by name.

functions.name: sendCommunication
functions.description: Call this when the user wants to draft and send an email related to a matter.

functions.name: handleAgentMessage
functions.description: Call this to route a conversational message through the Spaarke agent gateway.

functions.name: listAgentPlaybooks
functions.description: Call this to get playbooks available for the agent, optionally filtered by document type.

functions.name: runAgentPlaybook
functions.description: Call this to invoke a playbook on a document through the agent gateway.

functions.name: getAgentPlaybookStatus
functions.description: Call this to check the status of a long-running playbook started through the agent.

functions.name: createAnalysis
functions.description: Call this to start a deep document analysis that opens in the Analysis Workspace.

functions.name: getAnalysis
functions.description: Call this to check the status and results of a document analysis.

functions.name: getCurrentUser
functions.description: Call this to get the current user's profile and permissions.

functions.name: getUserCapabilities
functions.description: Call this to determine what features the current user has access to.
